description: Canonical AED lifecycle state registry. Each state has a category, evidence requirements, allowed next states, allowed and forbidden mutations, and authorization requirements. This is a registry of reporting vocabulary and policy expectations, not a runtime state machine.

NOT_RUN.description: Initial state before any governance work has begun for a given PR.
NOT_RUN.notes: PR-specific final-report labels like PR_395_MERGED and PR_396_MERGED are report labels, not canonical lifecycle states. They are produced from the terminal state PR_MERGED_AND_CLOSED_OUT and the merged PR number.

HOLD_MAIN_HEAD_MISMATCH.description: Stop. The observed origin/main HEAD does not match the expected head SHA for this PR run.
HOLD_MAIN_HEAD_MISMATCH.notes: Operator must reconcile origin/main before continuing. Do not push or merge.

HOLD_HEAD_CHANGED.description: Stop. The PR's headRefOid changed unexpectedly between the last verification and the next step.
HOLD_HEAD_CHANGED.notes: Re-fetch PR state and re-verify. Do not proceed with stale head.

HOLD_PR_CI_PENDING.description: CI is running or pending on the PR head. Operator must wait via bounded polling.
HOLD_PR_CI_PENDING.notes: Bounded polling only. No --watch. No unbounded loops.

HOLD_PR_CI_FAILED.description: A required CI check failed on the PR head. Do not merge.
HOLD_PR_CI_FAILED.notes: Operator should investigate and push a fix or report the failure.

HOLD_CODEX_RESPONSE_PENDING.description: A Codex review ping was posted; Codex has not yet responded within the bounded poll window.
HOLD_CODEX_RESPONSE_PENDING.notes: Bounded polling only. If poll budget is exhausted, remain in this state and report.

HOLD_NEW_CODEX_THREAD.description: Codex raised a new finding on the current head. Operator must patch or hold.
HOLD_NEW_CODEX_THREAD.notes: Do not resolve threads without explicit human authorization. Do not merge.

HOLD_NEW_ACTIVE_THREAD.description: A fresh unresolved active thread exists on the current head.
HOLD_NEW_ACTIVE_THREAD.notes: Do not resolve or merge. A thread is active if isResolved=false and isOutdated=false and commit.oid equals headRefOid.

CODEX_CLEAN_PASS_RESOLVE_ONLY_NEEDED.description: Codex clean-passed the current head. Any remaining unresolved threads are outdated. Operator must request explicit human authorization to resolve outdated threads before merge.
CODEX_CLEAN_PASS_RESOLVE_ONLY_NEEDED.notes: thread_resolve is permitted ONLY for threads where isOutdated=true. A complete thread inventory is required before any resolve-only authorization. The 14 preconditions in docs/stale_review_thread_auto_resolution_policy.md must also hold.

MERGE_READY_AWAITING_HUMAN_AUTHORIZATION.description: All preconditions for merge hold. Merge is permitted only after explicit human authorization with the exact live 40-character head SHA.
MERGE_READY_AWAITING_HUMAN_AUTHORIZATION.notes: Guarded squash merge with --match-head-commit is the only allowed mutation. The exact 40-character head SHA must appear in the human authorization phrase. The merge command must not include --admin or --auto. The branch may be deleted as part of the merge via --delete-branch only if the operator chose to include it; per-branch decisions are out of scope for this registry.

HOLD_MERGE_STATE_BLOCKED.description: mergeStateStatus is BLOCKED for a reason other than a Codex thread. Common causes: branch protection rules, required reviewers, or required status checks that the PR has not yet satisfied.
HOLD_MERGE_STATE_BLOCKED.notes: Investigate branch protection requirements before retrying. Do not bypass via --admin.

HOLD_PRE_MERGE_CONDITION_FAILED.description: Final pre-merge verification detected a condition failure. Do not merge.
HOLD_PRE_MERGE_CONDITION_FAILED.notes: Re-verify the exact live head SHA, CI, Codex, thread inventory, and mergeStateStatus before retrying.

HOLD_POST_MERGE_CI_PENDING.description: Post-merge main CI audit is pending. Operator must wait via bounded polling.

HOLD_POST_MERGE_CI_FAILED.description: Post-merge main CI failed. Closeout is not complete.
HOLD_POST_MERGE_CI_FAILED.notes: Investigate. Consider revert. Do not declare closeout complete.

HOLD_POST_MERGE_CI_NOT_OBSERVED.description: No post-merge main CI run was observed within the bounded poll window.
HOLD_POST_MERGE_CI_NOT_OBSERVED.notes: Re-check with a fresh bounded poll. Report if no run ever observed.

AUDIT_APPEND_SKIPPED_NEEDS_OPERATOR.description: The audit append step could not be performed safely because the helper or schema was ambiguous, or a previously appended audit entry was found to be malformed, non-canonical, incomplete, or suboptimal. The audit log is append-only; once an entry is appended, do not delete, trim, rewrite, or replace it unless the human operator explicitly authorizes that exact audit-log mutation. Operator must resolve the ambiguity without rewriting audit history. (Alias: AUDIT_APPEND_NEEDS_OPERATOR.)
AUDIT_APPEND_SKIPPED_NEEDS_OPERATOR.notes: Append-only closeout rule (codified 2026-06-10). If the audit validator or schema usage is ambiguous, the operator must (1) first run the repo-standard audit validator, (2) if validation fails, stop and report an audit hold, (3) if validation passes but the existing entry is non-canonical, do not rewrite it, (4) append a corrective follow-up entry only if the repo audit policy explicitly supports corrective entries, and (5) otherwise stop and report AUDIT_APPEND_NEEDS_OPERATOR (an alias of this state). Operator must run scripts/local/append_merge_action_audit.py with verified args. Do not invent arguments. Do not rewrite, trim, replace, or delete any already-appended audit row. Reference: docs/merge_action_audit_log.md §Append-only, docs/trace_policy_v1.md §6 Trace Completeness Rule. The alias name AUDIT_APPEND_NEEDS_OPERATOR is a reporting alias; this state is the single canonical entry.

PR_MERGED_PENDING_CLOSEOUT.description: PR is merged on origin/main. Post-merge main CI, audit append, and temp worktree cleanup are still pending.
PR_MERGED_PENDING_CLOSEOUT.notes: Only audit_append and worktree_remove are permitted mutations. Do not modify the primary worktree.

PR_MERGED_AND_CLOSED_OUT.description: PR is merged, post-merge main CI is green, audit entry is appended and validated, and the temp worktree is removed. No further mutations are required.
PR_MERGED_AND_CLOSED_OUT.notes: Terminal state. The PR-specific final-report label (e.g. PR_395_MERGED, PR_396_MERGED) is derived from this state plus the PR number, and is a report label rather than a canonical state in its own right.

HOLD_RESUME_CHECKPOINT_NEEDED.description: Continuation cannot safely determine the latest verified lifecycle state or the remaining permitted mutations from durable evidence. The operator must reconstruct state using read-only checks (current PR number and URL, current head SHA, current lifecycle state, completed phases, remaining permitted mutations, already-performed mutations, protected PR/worktree state) before any mutation is performed. Do not infer readiness from memory alone. Do not rerun broad workflows when a narrow continuation action is sufficient.
HOLD_RESUME_CHECKPOINT_NEEDED.notes: Resume checkpoint rule (codified 2026-06-10). Use this state when a continuation prompt lacks enough durable evidence to determine whether the next action is CI polling, Codex polling, thread resolution, merge, audit append, or cleanup. The operator must reconstruct state using read-only checks before proceeding. Policy-level prohibitions while in this state (encoded here as prose because they are not in the validator's canonical mutation vocabulary): do not perform a duplicate Codex ping, do not rewrite any already-appended audit row, and do not repeat an already-completed mutation. After the operator reconstructs the prior verified state, the next state is that prior non-terminal state; if merge is already verified complete, the next state is PR_MERGED_PENDING_CLOSEOUT; if closeout is already verified complete, the next state is PR_MERGED_AND_CLOSED_OUT. The canonical mutation vocabulary covers pr_merge, thread_resolve, comment_delete, review_dismiss, and force_push; the policy-level prohibitions (duplicate Codex ping, audit rewrite, repeated already-completed mutation) are documented here for the operator's reference. The validator will reject any token outside the canonical mutation vocabulary, so this entry uses the canonical tokens for the five audit-trail-prohibitions and the prose notes for the three policy-level prohibitions.
